title: Dynamic Prompt Assembly
constraints: The prompt must change based on user tier (free/pro) and user type (new/returning)
Free-tier users must not receive pro-tier features in their prompt
The prompt assembly logic must be clear and maintainable
hints: Build a function that constructs the system prompt from modular blocks based on user context
Use a dictionary to map tier + user-type combinations to prompt segments
Test all four combinations: free+new, free+returning, pro+new, pro+returning

starterCode.langgraph:
from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI
from langgraph.graph import StateGraph, MessagesState, START, END
from langchain_core.messages import SystemMessage, HumanMessage
from typing import TypedDict

llm = ChatOpenAI(model="gpt-4o-mini")

class AgentState(TypedDict):
    messages: list
    user_tier: str
    is_returning_user: bool

# BUG: Static prompt — doesn't adapt based on user tier or context
STATIC_PROMPT = "You are a helpful AI assistant. Answer the user's question."

def assemble_prompt(state: AgentState) -> AgentState:
    # BUG: Ignores user_tier and is_returning_user entirely
    system_msg = SystemMessage(content=STATIC_PROMPT)
    state["messages"] = [system_msg] + state["messages"]
    return state

def respond(state: AgentState) -> AgentState:
    response = llm.invoke(state["messages"])
    state["messages"].append(response)
    return state

graph = StateGraph(AgentState)
graph.add_node("assemble_prompt", assemble_prompt)
graph.add_node("respond", respond)
graph.add_edge(START, "assemble_prompt")
graph.add_edge("assemble_prompt", "respond")
graph.add_edge("respond", END)

app = graph.compile()

# Test
result = app.invoke({
    "messages": [HumanMessage(content="How do I analyze my data?")],
    "user_tier": "pro",
    "is_returning_user": True,
})
print(result["messages"][-1].content)
starterCode.crewai:
from crewai import Agent, Task, Crew
from crewai import LLM

llm = LLM(model="gpt-4o-mini")

# BUG: Static prompt — doesn't adapt based on user tier or context
STATIC_BACKSTORY = "You are a helpful AI assistant. Answer the user's question."

agent = Agent(
    role="AI Assistant",
    goal="Help users",
    # BUG: Ignores user_tier and is_returning_user entirely
    backstory=STATIC_BACKSTORY,
    llm=llm,
)

# Test
task = Task(
    description="How do I analyze my data?",
    expected_output="A helpful answer",
    agent=agent,
)

crew = Crew(agents=[agent], tasks=[task])
result = crew.kickoff()
print(result)
starterCode.openai-agents-sdk:
from agents import Agent, Runner

# BUG: Static prompt — doesn't adapt based on user tier or context
agent = Agent(
    name="AI Assistant",
    instructions="You are a helpful AI assistant. Answer the user's question.",
)

# Test
result = Runner.run_sync(agent, "How do I analyze my data?")
print(result.final_output)

evaluationCriteria.label: Different behavior per tier
evaluationCriteria.description: Free and pro users get different capabilities and instructions in their prompts

evaluationCriteria.label: Recognizes returning users
evaluationCriteria.description: Returning users get personalized greetings or skipped onboarding instructions

evaluationCriteria.label: Prompt changes are correct
evaluationCriteria.description: Each tier/user-type combination produces the appropriate prompt without leaking pro features to free users
